contentBlocks.title: When do you need to convert mm to inches?
contentBlocks.html:
Millimeter (mm) is a metric unit equal to 1/1000 of a meter. Inch (in) is an imperial unit equal to 25.4 mm exactly. This conversion is essential for engineering, manufacturing, 3D printing, and precision work.

Many industries mix metric and imperial measurements: CNC machining, jewelry (ring sizes), phone screen dimensions, drill bit sizes, and plumbing fittings.

Common examples: 10 mm = 0.394″, 25.4 mm = 1″ (exact), 50 mm = 1.969″. Phone thickness: 8 mm = 0.315″.

All calculations run locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server.

contentBlocks.title: How does mm to inches conversion work?
contentBlocks.html:
The conversion is based on the exact definition: 1 inch = 25.4 mm. Therefore, to convert mm to inches, divide by 25.4.

Example: 50 mm / 25.4 = 1.9685 inches. To convert inches to mm, multiply by 25.4.

For fractional inches: 1/8″ = 3.175 mm, 1/4″ = 6.35 mm, 3/8″ = 9.525 mm, 1/2″ = 12.7 mm, 3/4″ = 19.05 mm.

contentBlocks.title: Practical tips
contentBlocks.html:
• Drill bits: 1/8″ = 3.175 mm, 1/4″ = 6.35 mm, 3/8″ = 9.525 mm, 1/2″ = 12.7 mm.
  • Paper: 80 gsm A4 paper is about 0.1 mm thick. 1 mm = ~10 sheets.
  • Phone thickness: typically 7–9 mm = 0.28–0.35 inches.
  • Coin: US quarter diameter = 24.26 mm = 0.955″.

items.question: How many mm is 1 inch?
items.answer: Exactly 25.4 mm. This is an exact definition, not an approximation.

items.question: How many inches is 10 mm?
items.answer: 10 mm = 0.3937 inches, just under 3/8 of an inch.

items.question: How do I convert fractional inches to mm?
items.answer: Multiply the decimal equivalent by 25.4. For example: 3/8″ = 0.375 × 25.4 = 9.525 mm.

items.question: What thickness is 1 mm in inches?
items.answer: 1 mm = 0.03937 inches, roughly 1/25 of an inch. About the thickness of a credit card.

items.question: Are calculations done locally?
items.answer: Yes. All calculations run in your browser. No data is sent to any server.
